_comment: Sample CONFIRMED intent spec for the deterministic core. expectedComponentIds reference real node ids the scanner emits (function:<relPath>:<name> / file:<relPath>). The coverage-manifest capability now models reconcile at two honest levels: the per-span verdict primitive reconcileSpan (which EXISTS -> demonstrates a `satisfied` intent->code), and a cross-run orchestrator reconcilePriorRun (which genuinely does NOT exist anywhere in the index -> demonstrates an evidence-bound `missing` finding with a bounded missing-code proof). Run: node packages/core/runtime/assess-run.mjs --repo packages/core/src --intent examples/intent/assess-core.intent.json --out .assessment

capabilities.label: Honesty gate
capabilities.description: No finding may claim more than its evidence supports; absence must be proven and severity is capped by evidence strength.

processes.label: Cap severity by evidence strength
processes.invariant: unverifiable evidence caps at P2; inferred without a 2nd verified fact caps at P1

processes.label: Prove absence before claiming missing
processes.invariant: only `absent` (full coverage, residues ruled out) may assert true absence

capabilities.label: Coverage manifest
capabilities.description: Every assessable node carries an explicit coverage status; nothing is silently skipped.

processes.label: Account for every assessable node
processes.invariant: byStatus tallies must equal the number of assessable nodes

processes.label: Decide reuse vs rejudge for a single span
processes.invariant: a reformat (same normalized fingerprint) must reuse the prior verdict, not re-judge

processes.label: Reconcile a whole prior run across reformat-only changes
processes.invariant: loading a prior assessment graph must carry every still-valid verdict forward, re-judging only spans whose normalized fingerprint changed
